PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Rimpsyche conversations
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: an exchange that went somewhere - center the ease, friction, or topic that stuck, never reconstruct a transcript

instructions.li: Rimpsyche conversation - write what shifted between the two people: easier company, a snag, or a subject neither quite left behind
Rimpsyche conversation - use the supplied topic or outcome as evidence, but invent no quotation and no new confession
Rimpsyche conversation - keep ordinary talk as day texture unless the pawn's mood or relationship makes this one land harder

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: attentive to where a conversation left the two people

tones.li: socially observant, following ease or friction rather than exact words
quietly reflective, with the topic's afterweight still present

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: conversation afterfeel
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the afterfeel of a conversation - an opinion shifting, common ground, or the sting of being ignored

instructions.li: conversation afterfeel - write the one feeling still present after the exchange, not the conversation itself
conversation afterfeel - show whether the other person now feels nearer, farther away, or simply harder to read
conversation afterfeel - keep it to the supplied social consequence; add no new words or motives

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with the residue of a social exchange

tones.li: inward and lingering, an opinion still settling
briefly stung or warmed by how the other person responded
